div1.entryFree: Scan, to weigh in the mind, to consider with care: every word by all my wit being --ed, Err. II, 2, 152. which must be acted ere they may be --ed, Mcb. III, 4, 140. that would be --ed, Hml. III, 3, 75. I might entreat your honour to s. this thing no further, Oth. III, 3, 245. opinion's but a fool, that makes us s. the outward habit by the inward man, Per. II, 2, 56 (to examine, judge of, and explain the outward appearance by taking into account the qualities of the mind, with which it may perhaps have nothing to do).
